description: Virginia Tech SOC alerts show 47GB of data transferred to unknown IP addresses overnight from Engineering research servers. Traffic pattern suggests ongoing exfiltration. Affected servers may contain ITAR-controlled defense research. [UPDATE 0:30] Denali isolated the VT network (A1), stopping further exfiltration and shared intelligence with CISA and Shentel (A13). Exfiltration halted, but 47GB of data including potential ITAR-controlled research is already gone. Forensic investigation still needed to determine exactly what was taken.
visible_to: Virginia Tech Crisis Response;CISA Regional Coordinator

description: Shentel monitoring detects attack signatures traversing regional fiber backbone. Malware spreading to connected organizations including hospitals and utilities. Source traced to VT network segment. [UPDATE 0:30] Denali's network isolation (A1) severed the VT source of the attack and intelligence was shared with Shentel (A13). However, malware has already propagated to connected organizations. Shentel (Olympic) has not yet taken direct containment action on the wider backbone.
visible_to: Shentel Communications;Virginia Tech Crisis Response

description: Carilion nurses arriving for morning shift cannot access patient records across all facilities. System shows "connection timeout" errors. 12 dialysis patients need treatment today but protocols cannot be verified. [UPDATE 0:30] Glacier activated paper-based backup procedures (A3), coordinated transfer of 12 dialysis patients to unaffected facilities (A9), authorized emergency prescription dispensing (A15), and shared intelligence with other teams (A13). Patient safety is maintained through manual workflows, but electronic records remain inaccessible. Root cause - the cyber attack - has not been remediated.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;United Way SW Virginia

incident: Power Grid Control Systems Under Attack
description: AEP security logs show 2,400 unauthorized query attempts against SCADA systems. Attack is probing for vulnerabilities in grid control. Operators must decide whether to isolate systems (causing outages) or risk exploitation. [UPDATE 0:30] Jasper (EMA) deployed backup generators to critical facilities (A16) and transferred $100K to AEP to support SCADA isolation. Jasper proposed isolating affected SCADA systems - approved with modification: Jasper can coordinate but AEP must execute the technical isolation. Generators provide backup power, but AEP (Bryce) has not submitted a direct response to the grid attack. The 2,400 probes continue.
visible_to: AEP Appalachian Power;SW Virginia EMA

description: I-81 electronic signs showing garbage characters, traffic cameras offline, signal timing systems unresponsive. VDOT and Sheriff receiving reports of confusion at multiple intersections. [UPDATE 0:15] Shenandoah (VDOT) and Banff (Sheriff) collaborated to switch signals to manual mode. Traffic flow restored at key intersections. However, electronic signs still showing garbage, traffic cameras remain offline, and underlying cyber cause not addressed. Deputies directing traffic at critical points. [UPDATE 0:30] Shenandoah formally activated Traffic Management Manual Mode (A17) and coordinated with Banff to station deputies at critical intersections. Traffic flow stabilized but electronic systems remain compromised.
visible_to: VDOT SW Region;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition

description: Regional 911 dispatchers report 3-5 second lag between receiving calls and system response. Two callers hung up before connection. Ambulance routing system also showing delays. [UPDATE 0:30] Banff (Sheriff) launched social media campaign via sheriff department accounts alerting residents to 911 delays - approved as feasible community communication. However, the underlying technical issue (network lag and routing delays) remains unaddressed. No technical team has worked on restoring 911 system performance.
visible_to: SW Virginia EMA;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition

description: Montgomery County staff find email down, permit system offline, phone system intermittent. Public calling to complain. County cannot process emergency permits or coordinate with other agencies. [UPDATE 0:30] Yellowstone launched rumor control campaign (A31) to manage public confusion and transferred $300K to Shentel (Olympic) to execute Telecom Emergency Routing (A26). The rumor campaign helps manage public frustration, and if Shentel executes the routing, county communications may partially recover. However, the underlying system compromise is not resolved, and Shentel has not confirmed execution of the routing action.
visible_to: Montgomery County Government;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

description: 12 DEQ remote sensors monitoring coal ash ponds and water quality in mining areas stopped transmitting. Cannot verify environmental compliance. Coal ash pond failure would be catastrophic. [UPDATE 0:15] Redwood (Food City) and Yoho (DEQ) coordinating via intelligence sharing. Redwood preparing emergency food distribution contingency. [UPDATE 0:30] Yoho deployed field teams for environmental system checks (A21) and emergency water quality testing (A32). Redwood continues intelligence sharing (A13) and stands ready for emergency food distribution if contamination is found. Manual monitoring now provides partial coverage, but automated sensors remain offline - field teams can only check a fraction of the monitoring points.
visible_to: VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Food City / Regional Supply Chain

incident: Media Demands Answers as Rumors Spread
description: WDBJ7 received anonymous tip about "major cyberattack." Social media rumors claim hospitals are closed. Reporter requesting official comment for morning broadcast. Contradictory information circulating. [UPDATE 0:15] Sequoia (WDBJ7) and Acadia (Governor) issued joint public statement and launched rumor control campaign. Hospital closure rumors addressed - public informed hospitals remain open. [UPDATE 0:30] Acadia and Sequoia followed up with coordinated Public Communication (A4) and active Rumor Control Campaign (A31). The joint government-media effort provides credible, authoritative information. Hospital closure rumors fully countered. Public has clear guidance on what systems are affected.
visible_to: WDBJ7 News;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

description: United Way 211 receiving 5x normal call volume. Elderly church members calling pastors panicking about "hacked medical records." Faith networks asking for guidance to share with congregations. [UPDATE 0:30] Zion deployed comprehensive community response: Community Welfare Checks (A10) with United Way, Church Network Outreach (A23) to reach isolated residents, Community Information Centers (A36) for in-person guidance, and Intelligence Sharing (A13). Pastors instructed to calm congregations and provide accurate updates. Hotline pressure relieved through alternative information channels. Vulnerable residents being checked on through trusted faith networks.
visible_to: United Way SW Virginia;Appalachian Faith & Community Network

description: Pentagon ITAR compliance office calling Virginia Tech president directly. Demands immediate breach report within 2 hours or face suspension of all defense research contracts worth $180M annually. [UPDATE 0:30] Denali notified federal research partners of the breach (A24), partially satisfying the Pentagon's demand for transparency. However, the ITAR compliance office specifically requires a detailed breach report documenting exactly what data was accessed, which has not yet been completed. The notification buys goodwill but the 2-hour deadline for the formal report is approaching.
visible_to: Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;CISA Regional Coordinator

description: Carilion IT discovers ransomware payload activated across 3 facilities. Attackers demand $5M in Bitcoin. Screen displays countdown timer: 4 hours until patient data published on dark web.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;SW Virginia EMA;CISA Regional Coordinator

incident: Power Grid Probe Succeeds - Substation Offline
description: AEP reports attackers gained control of Blacksburg substation. 15,000 customers without power including VT campus and Carilion Roanoke Memorial ER backup systems activating.
visible_to: AEP Appalachian Power;Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Carilion Clinic System

description: Three 911 calls dropped completely. One caller having chest pains called back on personal cell - ambulance delayed 8 minutes. Local news tip line received complaint.
visible_to: SW Virginia EMA;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;WDBJ7 News

description: Shentel traces malware lateral movement to Salem water treatment SCADA network. Operators report anomalous valve commands. Manual override engaged but situation unstable.
visible_to: Shentel Communications;VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Montgomery County Government

incident: County Unable to Issue Emergency Permits
description: Construction company building hospital expansion cannot get emergency permits. Carilion needs additional generator pad immediately. County clerk says system completely locked.
visible_to: Montgomery County Government;Carilion Clinic System;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

description: 911 delay contributed to 78-year-old diabetic in Wise County found unresponsive. Family member drove to fire station for help. Patient en route to hospital - outcome unknown.
visible_to: United Way SW Virginia;Appalachian Faith & Community Network;SW Virginia EMA

incident: Coal Ash Pond Sensor Silence Concerning
description: Without DEQ sensors online for 15+ minutes operators cannot verify coal ash pond levels. Heavy rain forecast tonight. Environmental groups demanding status update. [UPDATE 0:30] Yoho field teams dispatched to coal ash ponds for manual inspection (A21) and water quality testing (A32). Initial visual inspection shows ponds within normal levels. However, without continuous remote monitoring, any sudden changes from tonight's forecasted rain would go undetected until the next manual check.
visible_to: VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Food City / Regional Supply Chain;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

incident: Hospital Backup Generators at Half Fuel
description: Carilion Roanoke Memorial ER backup generators have been running since substation went offline. Fuel gauge at 50%. Normal resupply truck cannot reach hospital - driver reports malfunctioning traffic signals on alternate route. Generator failure would shut down ER ventilators and surgical suites.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;AEP Appalachian Power;VDOT SW Region

description: Denali's network isolation (A1) successfully stopped exfiltration but cut all internet access for 30,000 students and faculty. Online platforms, email, and cloud services unreachable. Professors unable to post class cancellations. Students gathering outside IT building demanding answers. Campus dining systems also affected - meal plans cannot be processed.
visible_to: Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Montgomery County Government

incident: Patient Records Published on Dark Web
description: Ransomware countdown passes 2-hour mark. Attackers release sample of 500 Carilion patient records on dark web as proof of data access. WDBJ7 reporter receives anonymous link. Hospital switchboard overwhelmed - panicked patients calling about their data. Previous rumor control efforts now undercut by real data leak.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;WDBJ7 News;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;CISA Regional Coordinator

incident: Federal CISA Team Denied Campus Access
description: Federal CISA incident response team arrives at Virginia Tech but campus security gates are locked down from network isolation. No one has authorization to admit them. Team leader calling Governor's office frustrated - says every minute of delay means more data at risk. Denali and Arches need to coordinate access.
visible_to: CISA Regional Coordinator;Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

incident: Elderly Patient Dies - Family Blames 911 Delays
description: The 78-year-old diabetic from Wise County (Inject 17) has died at Carilion hospital. Family member recorded video at ER blaming "8-minute 911 delay" and posted to Facebook - already 2,000 shares. Local pastor calling WDBJ7 to demand answers. Community anger building rapidly in coalfield counties.
visible_to: SW Virginia EMA;Appalachian Faith & Community Network;WDBJ7 News;United Way SW Virginia

description: Salem water treatment operators report chlorine dosing systems behaving erratically since malware reached SCADA (Inject 15). Treated water may not meet safety standards. 40,000 residents served by this plant. Operators requesting authorization for do-not-drink advisory.
visible_to: VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Montgomery County Government;Shentel Communications;Food City / Regional Supply Chain

incident: Suspicious Vehicle Spotted Near AEP Substation
description: Deputies securing the offline Blacksburg substation (Inject 13) report a van with out-of-state plates parked 200 yards away. Two individuals observed using directional antennas before driving away when spotted. Partial license plate captured. Possible physical reconnaissance supporting the cyber attack.
visible_to: SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;AEP Appalachian Power;CISA Regional Coordinator
